title: navarretia torreyella
intro: Navarretia torreyella is a rare and endangered plant species native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the states of California, Nevada, and Arizona. It belongs to the phlox family and is known for its fragrant white flowers and distinctive leaf shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Navarretia torreyella is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are said to promote healing, reduce pain, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
